---
title: "About"
description: "Author, citation, and license."
---

## Author

**Félix Leiva** — academic researcher and the author of ordena[R]{.brand-r}.

- GitHub: [@felixpleiva](https://github.com/felixpleiva)

## Citation

If ordena[R]{.brand-r} supports your work, please cite it:

> Leiva, F. P. (2026). *ordena[R]{.brand-r}: a free, open-source tool for managing author lists in academic papers* (Version 1.0.0) [Software]. <https://felixpleiva.github.io/ordenaR/>

The repository ships a [`CITATION.cff`](https://github.com/felixpleiva/ordenaR/blob/main/CITATION.cff) file in [Citation File Format v1.2.0](https://citation-file-format.github.io/), so GitHub will display a **"Cite this repository"** button at the top right of the [repo page](https://github.com/felixpleiva/ordenaR) and offer BibTeX/APA conversions automatically.

A BibTeX entry:

```bibtex
@software{leiva_ordenaR_2026,
  author       = {Leiva, F{\'e}lix P.},
  title        = {{ordenaR}: A free, open-source tool for managing author lists in academic papers},
  year         = {2026},
  version      = {1.0.0},
  url          = {https://felixpleiva.github.io/ordenaR/},
  note         = {MIT licensed}
}
```

## License

- **Code** — [MIT License](https://github.com/felixpleiva/ordenaR/blob/main/LICENSE). Use it in your lab, fork it, modify it, ship it. Attribution is appreciated but not legally required.
- **Documentation** (this site, README, etc.) — [CC-BY 4.0](https://github.com/felixpleiva/ordenaR/blob/main/LICENSE-docs). Re-use freely with attribution.

## Reporting issues

GitHub Issues: <https://github.com/felixpleiva/ordenaR/issues>.
